title: Synthetic Enhancer Design Pipeline
subtitle: Enhancer design links sequence edits, regulatory-track prediction, cell-type specificity, and experimental caution.
summary: The HiC-TAD project sketches a pipeline using AlphaGenome predictions, CNN training, motif insertion, and validation loops.

artifact.title: Predicted regulatory-track response
artifact.description: Change the deletion size index to see how regulatory tracks might remain stable or become disrupted.

contentBlocks.text: Design Is a Loop, Not a Single Prediction

A synthetic enhancer pipeline proposes sequence changes, predicts regulatory effects, checks cell-type specificity, filters for safety and off-target structure, then returns to sequence design. The model is a guide; biology still gets the final vote.

contentBlocks.content:
Nominate locusChoose a region from chromatin structure, expression, or variant evidence.
Propose sequenceAdd, remove, or optimize motifs.
Predict tracksCompare CTCF, ATAC, histone marks, and expression effects.
ValidatePrioritize experiments rather than replacing them.

sections.heading: What AlphaGenome Adds
body: AlphaGenome-style models predict many regulatory outputs from long DNA sequence context. That matters because an enhancer's effect is not just local motif grammar; it depends on surrounding sequence and cell type.
For design, the useful object is the delta: what changed between the original and edited sequence across regulatory tracks.

sections.heading: Why Structure Matters
body: An enhancer that raises a desired signal may still be wrong if it disrupts chromatin boundaries or activates in the wrong cell type.
That is why this project connects enhancer design with Hi-C/TAD analysis: sequence function and 3D genome organization are entangled.

pitfalls: Treating predicted enhancer strength as experimental truth.
Optimizing one track while damaging another.
Forgetting that non-coding edits can affect chromatin structure.

quiz.question: What is the main use of model predictions in enhancer design?
options: Prioritizing and filtering candidates before experiments
quiz.explanation: Predictions guide candidate selection; experiments remain necessary for validation.
